item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I smell a conspiracy. Well written. +1

+ Spoilers!
- hide block

Image caption: "[…] after the completion of water egress training."
Recovery Report: "[…] possibly a result of the astronauts' lack of water egress training."
Even without all the things Bill pointed out last page, this alone immediately shouted "foul play" at me.

Seems like O5-9 sensed something was off and got assimilated for it. Poor bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I've found a lot of evidence of shit going down but it doesn't add up into a coherent picture.

+ show block
– hide block

Much ado is made of the astronauts having completed their water egress training. That's important later.

So the first blatant individual strangeness is Yates suddenly going "This place is spotless, just a bit dusty though." Catani goes 'wut' and he replies "I said it's… nothing, nothing. It's nothing." This seems more like mind overwriting or something than an impostor though and they haven't even found the mirrors yet? And what's with the scribbles?

There's a plasma signal image, but I have no clue what the bloody hell it's supposed to mean, it looks like it's some kind of magically-imaged side view of the place, showing that the long hall they wandered down not only wasn't there before they arrived on September 13, but that it isn't connected to the rest of the structure?

Searching the internet only suggested blood plasma structural readings so idk what else I'm supposed to be getting here.

Anyway the black shiny stuff the walls are made of is invincible. Important later.

The radio graph thing is uninformative due to lack of axis labels aside from something appearing mirrored around a particular frequency. And I'm not sure if that's even correct for how radio works but again I know nada there.

Next up is the mirror crystal breaking. I dont know if they passed through a mirror frame or not though, dialogue was ambiguous.

Next big thing is Catani. After one smash, Catani is no longer right with Yates where he should have been. After a second, something else with Catani's voice but imperfect mannerisms spooks the heck out of Yates; impostor Catani always calls him "Edward" instead of "Ed" but Yates seems to miss this. The real Catani tries to warn Yates about the impostor but his signal is mostly drowned out by the impostor save for the very end ("-sten to it!", probably "Don't listen to it"). The real Catani catches back up, but the impostor has already claimed his position so Yates shoots the real one with… whatever it is they have 'for defense'.

Oh, also, at some point Yates heard that song again.

Next thing we see, imposters of Yates and Catani knock on the door without warning after Milner calls for them. The imposters both say "everything perfect". They didn't shoot the flare like they were supposed to if audio cut out, much like impostor Catani earlier didn't notify Yates before suddenly spooking him.

The medical diagnostics of the crew at the day 36 check-in are nothing but the musical signal instead of the expected medical stuff. In spite of this one of the crew transmits "Albinoni Adagio?". Not Albinoni's Adagio.

Then there's the re-entry process where the crew acts nutters, or at least impostor Catani does. The brain wave readings tell us that SOMETHING happened and their brains flatlined at some point - when, why, and how, is unclear. Heart rates are constant but I dont know if those are constant comatose or constant normal active or what.

Finally the O5 Council (or more likely just O5-9 since that's who they report to) seals the recovery records and leaves a summary full of BS for reasons unclear. Security? Sabotage? Impostor on the O5 Council? I cant tell.

Earlier the article said they were supposed to meet up with another independent ship to bring them home but that seems to have not happened? Pilot capsule hatch was opened immediately. The summary blames this on not completing their water egress training which they very much completed earlier in the article. Then it blames psychological instability because of a transmission seven minutes after landing where we hear Catani and Yates going "get us out of here" and [we] "were trapped" (possibly misinterpreted). Notably, we don't know whether that seven minutes passed before or after the Foundation teams showed up and dug them out.

The spacesuits, much like the walls in the structure, are invincible. All 3 impostors. Then the bodies are to be cremated (despite two verbally wanting burial last anyone heard), presumably so that the bodies can be disappeared with no suspicion… despite the fridge logic of trying to burn the invincible suits.

There's a ton of odd turns of phrase used in the summary that make no sense. "Despite the stray from flight pattern deviations", "was a resounding success and provided fruitful in gaining invaluable data on SCP-5420", stuff like that.

The odd thing is that despite Yates shooting the real Catani with an unspecified weapon, the capsule transmission had Yates and Catani trying to get out instead of Yates and Milner. And Milner, how did he even get replaced? He was going to need to use the extra thrusters to maneuver if they didnt get there on time and they only got there right on time. Flight path deviations were likely imperfect handling of controls on the part of the impostors so how did they even have time to swap out Milner?

Theres a bunch of minor errors (Yates and Milners lines being swapped with aye dont forget about me and "unfortunately", Alan being spelled with two Ls in one spot) but I dunno wtf is going on besides "impostors?" and maybe "learn from radio?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a GREAT post. Some of my favorite articles are related to space. +1
Also, what was the code you used on this article? I want to use this on my own draft. Please respond to this if you can! I want to know this format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have some theorys:

1-Foundation is keeping something hidden.

2-Lancer crew did some sort of "realitiy jump" since some of the things Lancer crew said do not match.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was alright, immersion was kinda ruined for me by the cassini picture but other then that its ok. wish it was elaborated more on what exactly happened, it's not like the foundation to not keep the bodies for study especially since they seem anomalous. no vote for now.

edit: after thinking about it a bit more i remembered that they wanted to be buried, not cremated. now i'm a lot more interested! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to hear that floppy, thank you a lot for critting this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
minor crit:

As SCP-5420's orbit is outside Mars orbit,

should probably be "As SCP-5420's orbit is outside the orbit of Mars,"

It it a place to live, not a place to die.

should probably be "It is a place…"

Pop a flare found

should probably be "Pop a flare round"? This was confusing to me

there's also some inconsistency with regards to "Probe 7B" vs "Probe 7-B"

otherwise its really good, although a tad bit similar to 4555, still a hearty +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to have critted this, I love me a good space anomaly & mystery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bold of you to assume I'm smart enough to figure out what happened! +1 anyways lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've got a weak spot for space-themed SCPs, and despite it's length it plays out well and was an enjoyable read.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Meta structure - dead ahead!

A smaller space skip from me, JackalRelated. Many thanks to everyone who provided crit!
This one's inspired by the 70s sci-fi/horror show, Space: 1999 and it's aesthetics.

Writing music: here.

Oh, and yes, it's entirely possible to determine what happened to the Lancer II crew; there's a definite answer that the skip provides. All it takes is close reading, internal cross-referencing, and a little bit of your imagination. Good hunting!
